visualization.title: Total Scholarship Amount by Student (highest recipients)

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT s.id AS student_id,
       s.first_name || ' ' || s.last_name AS student_name,
       s.index_number AS student_index_number,
       SUM(sc.amount) AS total_scholarship_amount
FROM public.students AS s
JOIN public.scholarships AS sc
  ON sc.student_id = s.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.first_name, s.last_name, s.index_number
ORDER BY total_scholarship_amount DESC, s.id;

issues: Invalid Vega-Lite color encoding: the spec uses "color": {"datum":"#4C78A8"}. For a constant color use "color": {"value":"#4C78A8"}; the current form will likely cause the chart to fail to render.

Minor/optional: the x-axis sort uses {"field":"total_scholarship_amount","op":"sum","order":"descending"} although total_scholarship_amount is already an aggregated field. This is not fatal but redundant; you can sort by the y encoding or use a simple descending sort.
Warning: student_name (first + last) is used as the nominal x field; if names are not unique, bars could be ambiguous. Consider using student_id as a key or ensuring name uniqueness if that matters.
The SQL and chart mapping (aggregated total per student, ranking by total) match the question, but the Vega-Lite spec contains an invalid color encoding that will break rendering.

visualization.title: Total Scholarship Amount by Student (highest recipients)

result.sql:
SELECT s.id AS student_id,
       s.first_name || ' ' || s.last_name AS student_name,
       s.index_number AS student_index_number,
       SUM(sc.amount) AS total_scholarship_amount
FROM public.students AS s
JOIN public.scholarships AS sc
  ON sc.student_id = s.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.first_name, s.last_name, s.index_number
ORDER BY total_scholarship_amount DESC, s.id;

issues: Invalid Vega-Lite color encoding: the spec uses "color": {"datum":"#4C78A8"}. For a constant color use "color": {"value":"#4C78A8"}; the current form will likely cause the chart to fail to render.

Minor/optional: the x-axis sort uses {"field":"total_scholarship_amount","op":"sum","order":"descending"} although total_scholarship_amount is already an aggregated field. This is not fatal but redundant; you can sort by the y encoding or use a simple descending sort.
Warning: student_name (first + last) is used as the nominal x field; if names are not unique, bars could be ambiguous. Consider using student_id as a key or ensuring name uniqueness if that matters.
The SQL and chart mapping (aggregated total per student, ranking by total) match the question, but the Vega-Lite spec contains an invalid color encoding that will break rendering.
